questions.question: A parabola has vertex at (2, −3) and focus at (2, 1). Write the equation of the parabola in the form (x − h)² = 4p(y − k).
questions.explanation: The vertex is (h, k) = (2, −3). Since focus and vertex have the same x-coordinate, this is a vertical parabola. The distance p = |1 − (−3)| = 4, so 4p = 16. The equation is (x − 2)² = 16(y + 3).

questions.question: An ellipse has foci at (±5, 0) and the sum of distances from any point on the ellipse to the two foci is 26. Find the length of the minor axis.
questions.explanation: For an ellipse, 2a = 26, so a = 13. The distance from center to each focus is c = 5. Using b² = a² − c², we get b² = 169 − 25 = 144, so b = 12. The minor axis has length 2b = 24.

questions.question: A hyperbola has the equation (y − 2)²/36 − (x + 1)²/64 = 1. Find the distance between its two vertices.
questions.explanation: This is a vertical hyperbola with center (−1, 2). The value a² = 36, so a = 6. The vertices are at (−1, 2 + 6) and (−1, 2 − 6), giving distance 2a = 12.

questions.question: A parabola has equation y = −2x² + 12x − 13. Rewrite it in vertex form y = a(x − h)² + k and identify the focus.
questions.explanation: Complete the square: y = −2(x² − 6x) − 13 = −2(x − 3)² + 18 − 13 = −2(x − 3)² + 5. Vertex is (3, 5). Here a = −2, so 4p = −1/2, giving p = −1/8. Focus is 1/8 unit below vertex: (3, 5 − 1/8) = (3, 39/8).

questions.question: An ellipse has equation 9x² + 16y² = 144. A point P on the ellipse has x-coordinate 2. Find the y-coordinate(s) of P.
A hyperbola has asymptotes y = ±(3/4)x and passes through the point (5, 6). Find the equation of the hyperbola in standard form (centered at origin).
questions.explanation: For a horizontal hyperbola x²/a² − y²/b² = 1, asymptotes are y = ±(b/a)x, so b/a = 3/4. Substitute (5, 6): 25/a² − 36/b² = 1. From b = 3a/4, we get 25/a² − 36·16/(9a²) = 1 → 25/a² − 64/a² = 1 → −39/a² ≠ 1. Recalculate: (25/a²) − (36·16)/(9a²) simplifies to a² = 25, b² = 16.
